title: quercus × byarsii
intro: Quercus × byarsii, also known as the Byars oak, is a hybrid tree species resulting from the cross between Quercus alba (white oak) and Quercus coccinea (scarlet oak). The bark and leaves of the Byars oak have been traditionally used in herbal medicine, exhibiting astringent and anti-inflammatory properties due to the presence of tannins and flavonoids. The medicinal properties of Quercus × byarsii can help alleviate symptoms of diarrhea, fever, and respiratory issues, while also offering potential health benefits such as antioxidant and immune-boosting effects. The herbal preparations of the Byars oak typically involve infusion, decoction, or tincture of its bark and leaves, which can be used to create teas, salves, or extracts for various health applications.
